D10 Imaging L4 Analyze Reference

Photon Shot Noise

light arrives as Poisson photons — collect N and you get √N noise, so signal-to-noise improves only with the square root of exposure

Photons arrive as a Poisson process: the variance equals the mean. Collect N photons on average per pixel and the noise on each measurement has standard deviation √N. Relative noise is √N / N = 1/√N — so quadrupling the photons only halves the noise. This is the fundamental noise floor every camera fights, no matter how good its electronics.

Drag the photon count from a starlit handful to bright daylight. At the low end the patch is visibly grainy; at the high end it is smooth, and the SNR readout doubles every time you quadruple the photons.
